title: Special Use Permit, EV Charging Grant Missed, and Good Cause Eviction Hearing Announced
summary: Multi-topic segment. (1) Jake Dishaw presents item 44: special use permit for neighborhood market at 401 Pond Street, approved by planning commission with conditions for storefront improvements. Councilor Nave voices conditional support. (2) Item 45: Parkside Commons zone change R5 to MX2 approved by planning commission to eliminate split-zone parcel. (3) Extended EV charging follow-up: Commissioner Michael Collins reports the JCOM grant opportunity was missed because the RFP requirement could not be met in time. Owen Kernney contacted alternative vendors but none could provide the full package. One ongoing grant remains but requires ~$170K city match. Councilor Nave expresses disappointment at lost revenue. Caldwell asks if JCOM could still execute; Collins confirms yes but notes unresolved agreement issues. (4) Good Cause Eviction public hearing announced for tomorrow in town hall format with sign-up cards; Councilors Jones-Rowser and Paniagua to co-chair. (5) Code enforcement concerns raised about trash at West Gifford liquor store and parking at Brighton/Canon corner store.

political_signal.description: Council grapples with consequences of EV charging RFP decision; Councilor Nave expresses disappointment at missed revenue opportunity while respecting equity concerns

processing_notes: JCOM grant deadline passed; city missed $400K+ infrastructure opportunity due to RFP timeline conflict
Owen Kernney contacted alternative vendors per council request; none could provide full service package
Good Cause Eviction public hearing scheduled for Feb 19 in town hall format
Amanda (council secretary) departing for county position after earning master's degree
Segment 19 of 19 from Common Council Study Session, Wednesday February 18th 2026
Transcript corrections applied: national grid -> National Grid
Potential duplicate detected: overlaps with CTX-COUNCIL-2026-00106 -- linked, not skipped
